pattern: A tree knapsack where each node returns, per amount spent, the best subtree profit for the two cases of the node buying (children get the half-price discount) or not buying.
hints: Whether an employee gets the half-price discount depends only on whether their direct boss buys, so the decision propagates down the tree.
For each subtree compute, for every possible amount of budget spent inside it, the maximum profit, in two flavors: the node bought its stock or it did not.
Merge children with a bounded knapsack over the budget; a node that buys passes the discounted flavor to its children and adds its own profit, otherwise it passes the full-price flavor.

edge_inputs.description: boss buys, employee gets the discount
budget too small to buy both, take the better one
two children, discount makes both worthwhile
single employee whose stock loses money is skipped
tight budget forces choosing fewer buyers

stress.description: random rooted tree with small prices and a moderate budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 11))
    present = [rng.randrange(1, 51) for _ in range(n)]
    future = [rng.randrange(1, 51) for _ in range(n)]
    hierarchy = []
    for v in range(2, n + 1):
        u = rng.randrange(1, v)
        hierarchy.append([u, v])
    budget = rng.randrange(1, 40)
    return [n, present, future, hierarchy, budget]
stress.note: kept to <=11 employees so the subset-brute oracle stays fast
